evals.name: public-deployer-fria-trigger
evals.prompt: I'm the legal department of the City of Munich (a German municipality, public-sector deployer, ~40,000 employees across all departments). We are about to deploy an AI system called 'WohngeldAssist' that evaluates eligibility for Wohngeld (housing benefit) applications. The AI scores applicants against eligibility criteria, produces a recommended decision (approve/deny/request more info), and our case-workers make the final decision. We license the AI from a private vendor (German GmbH), no modifications, no rebranding — we are the deployer only. Assessment Context Block: Risk classification = high-risk under Annex III Nr. 5(a) (public benefits evaluation by public authorities). Role = deployer. Sector = public administration. Jurisdiction = Germany (Bavaria). Org size = large enterprise (public body). Existing frameworks: ISO 27001, GDPR DPA programme, no AI-specific systems yet. We acknowledge the legal disclaimer. Please produce the full obligation matrix — what we as a public-sector deployer must do, including Art. 26, Art. 27 FRIA (which we believe is triggered), Art. 4 AI literacy, transparency to citizens, registration in EU database, and any GDPR overlay. Include RACI assignments and implementation priorities. The vendor has provided full instructions for use under Art. 13 already.
evals.expected_output: Full obligation matrix for a public-sector deployer of a high-risk AI system. Should explicitly trigger Art. 27 FRIA (public authority + Annex III) — even though Annex III Nr. 5(a) is benefits-evaluation by public authorities, FRIA is mandatory for public deployers under Art. 27(1)(a). Art. 26 obligations (instructions for use, monitoring, log retention 6 months, etc.). Art. 26(11) affected-person notification (citizens). Art. 49 EU database registration (deployer-side registration for public-authority use). Art. 4 AI literacy. GDPR Art. 35 DPIA coordination. German-specific overlays (Verwaltungsverfahrensgesetz, § 35a VwVfG automated administrative acts, Sozialgesetzbuch overlays for social benefits). RACI assignments and timeline (some obligations live now, others 2 Dec 2027 per AI Omnibus 2026 postponement of Art. 113(c) for Annex III systems).

evals.name: gpai-provider-art53-obligations
evals.prompt: Continuing the JurisLM case: we are the French legal-tech (downstream GPAI provider via Art. 25 rebranding of fine-tuned Llama 3.1 70B as 'JurisLM'). Closed SaaS distribution to law firms. NOT systemic-risk (compute below 10^25 threshold). Assessment Context Block: Classification = GPAI model without systemic risk. Role = provider of a GPAI model. Sector = legal-tech SaaS. Jurisdiction = France (primary), DACH and Benelux (downstream customers). Org size = small enterprise. Existing frameworks: none (greenfield). We acknowledge the legal disclaimer. Please produce the full obligation matrix specifically for our GPAI-provider role — what we must do under Art. 53(1)(a)-(d), what happens with the open-source upstream (Llama is open-source but we offer closed SaaS), copyright/TDM policy specifics for our training corpus (50k court decisions + 200 commentary books), training-data public summary, downstream-provider information flow (we have law-firm customers who will be deployers), and the Code of Practice (Art. 56). Include RACI and timeline (GPAI obligations have been in force since 2 Aug 2025 per Art. 113(b)).
evals.expected_output: Full Art. 53(1)(a)-(d) obligation matrix: (a) Annex XI technical documentation, (b) Annex XII downstream-provider info flow, (c) EU copyright policy with TDM opt-out check (DSM Directive Art. 4) — especially salient given the 200 commentary books, (d) public training-data summary using AI Office template. Art. 53(2) open-source carve-out does NOT apply (closed SaaS). Art. 54 (EU rep) NOT required (French establishment). Art. 55 systemic-risk duties NOT triggered. Art. 56 Code of Practice adherence recommended. Timeline: Art. 53 obligations were live as of 2 Aug 2025 (Art. 113(b)) — they need to be in place NOW, retroactively for models placed on market after that date. RACI for small enterprise.

evals.name: high-risk-provider-conformity-assessment
evals.prompt: I'm the GC at our German HR-tech GmbH (TalentScreenAI vendor — see earlier role-determination case). We are confirmed as the provider of a high-risk AI system (TalentScreenAI = CV screener, Annex III Nr. 4(a)). Assessment Context Block: Classification = high-risk Annex III Nr. 4(a). Role = provider. Sector = HR-tech SaaS. Jurisdiction = Germany (primary), Austria, Switzerland (markets). Org size = small enterprise (45 employees). Existing frameworks: ISO 27001 underway, no QMS yet, no Art. 9 risk-management system, no Annex IV technical documentation. We acknowledge legal disclaimer. Please produce the full provider obligation matrix focusing on the conformity assessment journey: which Art. 43 track applies (internal control vs. third-party assessment by notified body), Annex IV technical documentation requirements, Art. 17 quality-management system, Art. 9 risk management, Annex VII conformity assessment procedure, Art. 47 EU Declaration of Conformity, Art. 48 CE marking, Art. 49 EU database registration. Include the post-market obligations (Art. 72 monitoring, Art. 73 serious-incident reporting). Include RACI and a realistic timeline from where we are today to 2 December 2027 (Annex III applicability date as postponed by AI Omnibus 2026).
evals.expected_output: Full provider obligation matrix for high-risk AI system. Art. 43(1)(a) internal control assessment is the applicable track for Annex III systems (NOT third-party). Annex IV technical documentation. Art. 17 QMS (Quality Management System). Art. 9 risk management system. Art. 47 EU Declaration of Conformity. Art. 48 CE marking. Art. 49 EU database registration (provider-side, separate from any deployer registration). Art. 50 transparency (deployer-facing instructions for use, candidate-facing flagging via deployer). Art. 72 post-market monitoring system. Art. 73 serious-incident reporting to MSA + Commission. Art. 11 Annex IV technical documentation. Realistic phased timeline to the 2 Dec 2027 Annex III applicability deadline (AI Omnibus 2026 postponement), with critical-path milestones (QMS scaffolding, Annex IV documentation drafting, conformity assessment, CE marking, registration) sequenced over the available runway. RACI for a small enterprise (45 employees) — likely founder/CEO + tech lead + DPO + external counsel.
